title: Love and Eva
wikitext:
[]: Love and Eva             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Sun Jun 01, 2008 10:43 pm I'm surprised to find that Everlasting love thread still alive and at so many pages. So Evageeks, it's time to talk about ourselves, so let's take a seat on the mats and get in a circle. The word love get's thrown around quite a bit on these boards. So, how does Eva and love go together for you, love for the show, love for the characters? How does it feel and how do you react to it? How does it compare to other kinds of love?

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sun Jun 01, 2008 11:00 pm Evangelion did something very strange for me, especially character wise. There are a lot of anime that i will say "that guy is bad ass" "that girl is really hot" and stuff like that. But with Evangelion i can honestly say that i love certain characters. Maybe because Anno created characters that were so deep and complex that we feel we know these people in real life. We all know or feel we are a Shinji. We all know a girl or heard of a girl like Asuka. Its enough for me to truly hope even knowing these characters are fictional that they in the end become happy. I truly hope that Shinji did find happiness, that Asuka did get over her issues and became happy. Its a feeling that most fiction probably has as its goal but never attains. So i guess thats a kind of love.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jun 01, 2008 11:38 pm NGE came at a time in my life where I simply needed it. I was equal parts Shinji and Asuka, and even Rei to a degree. I had been through the same metaphoric hell, to the point I didn't want to live either and everything was just too painful. It came at a time when I was utterly lost.... like floating aimlessly in an endless ocean. And NGE came a long and gave me hope. It showed me a version of my life and said "you can find a way to make it through this." It's remarkable how art when it's honest, passionate, and at its purest in being a representation of another's life can stir a person to that degree. But beyond all of the personal importance it also turned out to be a work I've come to respect above all others because of the extremely high level of craftsmanship Anno put into it. It reinvigorated my passion for film and film-craft. I'm not saying that I'll never find a work that combines that extremely powerful poignancy and profundity and craftsmanship, but nothing has come close yet.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Jun 02, 2008 11:59 am Completely agree with above statements. I was depressed for awhile, no friends, no reason for doing anything, I was just there taking up space. After watching a few episodes of Eva, I'm like hey this is pretty cool, but it's just a show. Obviously the more I watched the more I read into it. But what really slapped me in the face and said "Hey! You're the one not doing anything, you're the one who's sitting there feeling sorry for yourself." was the TV ending, Episodes 25 and 26. It analyzed the characters and I could always find a little bit of myself in everyone of them. I watched them and really took to heart what was going on. And at the end when they're all saying "Congratulations", well it still brings a tear to my eye because I know I can be happy if I want to be. Also I'm very happy that I found these forums. I don't have many friends and the few I do have I can't talk about anything Evangelion to them because to them it's some stupid show. So I am very happy for this place and the people on here.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Jun 02, 2008 4:09 pm Within various points in my life, I would sit down in a somewhat comfortable chair and realize that some of the Evangelion characters (in my mind) are like me. So, with the appropriate media, I would unzip the fly apparatus and pull out my massive dong, to pump my load all over Asuka's tender and not-so-innocent breasts, or perhaps take Shinji's anal virginity in an attempt to make him a man. That's how I came to love Neon Genesis Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Mon Jun 02, 2008 6:49 pm I'm workin on my post for this one, but Jimbo's post in the other thread made me want to write more, so I'll probobly post it later after I've thought more about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 02, 2008 7:58 pm Yojimbo wrote: I was equal parts Shinji and Asuka, and even Rei to a degree.

[]:             Quote Post by PartyHard » Mon Jun 02, 2008 10:45 pm Ironfoot wrote: So, with the appropriate media, I would unzip the fly apparatus and pull out my massive dong, to pump my load all over Asuka's tender and not-so-innocent breasts, or perhaps take Shinji's anal virginity in an attempt to make him a man.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Jun 03, 2008 12:21 am I first remember watching Eva, and thinking, 'This is a cool show'. The last few episodes really confused me, and I threw myself into trying to figure it out. I don't really think I figured much out until I had my first bout with depression, and all of a sudden, it clicked, and made a lot of sense to me. I guess that's really how the show speaks to people, not through words that a normally functioning mind would try to interpret, but through ideas that a person who's going through a really tough time in their life will piece together.

[]:             Quote Post by Master Hand » Tue Jun 03, 2008 12:48 am When I first saw Eva which was the first 2 episodes part of Toonami's robot week on cartoonetwork,I thought Misato was cute which is true.I remember that scene with Shinji and his father.Eva 01 getting beaten by Sachiel,etc.Then a few years later,I see episode 1 again and see the same parts I saw a few years back and think.Is this the same thing?Turns out it was.Then I got the DVD for the first 5 episodes on my 16th Birthday and decided to get the other ones which I thought werent out.So,the next few months I got the rest of the episodes.I knew I loved Evangelion from watching my first DVD.Kind of religious for me but hey,it's a badass anime.I just love it and cant wait for Rebuild to come out.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Sat Jun 07, 2008 10:58 am Love means I love it; no other way to put it. I don't believe the series has ever taught me anything about human love, not even the love of the self, because my quest for that is ongoing. But the love of being entertained, of being intellectually stimulated, that is present in spades. I love the series for doing that for me, for becoming one of my favourite things in the whole world. And, I suppose I would say that I "love" Shinji. Not in the sense that I'm "in love" with him, or love him unreservedly...but that I love him as a fictional creation, as someone who resonates with me emotionally, a "figure" in my life whose influence I can't yet gauge, but is complex. I love him for that.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Jun 07, 2008 11:16 am I was in the aftermath of my major depression issues, and I unconsciously needed something final to prove to myself that life was worth living. Evangelion popped in, and I totally fell in love with the characters because of how much I could relate to the characters, most prominently Rei and Kaworu. If you ever see me in my house, I almost NEVER talk at all. NEVER. Unless someone is talking to me. Almost all of the characters were somewhat screwed up, and I almost immediately knew that I really found a treasure. I loved how the series didn't just go for the sex appeal and that cheesy bullshit of stating the obvious all the time (IT'S ATTACKING THE BUILDING! OH MY GOD IT'S HORRIBLE! IT'S ATTACKING ANOTHER BUILDING! WE CAN'T STOP THIS THING!) and instead asking questions about philosophy and existence and human emotions and self-control. But favoring Rei and Kaworu does not mean that I don't connect with the others. I just loved those two characters because they had so much to offer philosophy-wise without analyzing their behavior, aka, they spoke what they believed. Shinji, Asuka, are still in my heart, and I never fail to think about all of them when I have free time.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jun 07, 2008 2:09 pm ...don't we alredy have this thread by different name? Also, "Love and Eva" forces EVERY time I see it thinking this is thread about how hard love and Evangelion is to be combined.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Jun 07, 2008 3:49 pm Xard wrote: ...don't we alredy have this thread by different name? Also, "Love and Eva" forces EVERY time I see it thinking this is thread about how hard love and Evangelion is to be combined.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jun 07, 2008 3:56 pm Haha, yeah I mean first time I saw this thread I thought this was going to be "has Evangelion affected your relationships? It is easier for you to act with love interests or are you scaring them away with geekery?" Ha! Found it!
